speaker: Host
text_en: Undeclared wars are commonplace. Tragically, our government engages in preemptive war, otherwise known as aggression, with no complaints from the American people.
text: 未宣战的战争早已司空见惯。可悲的是，我们的政府发动先发制人的战争，也就是所谓的侵略，而美国民众对此几乎毫无怨言。

speaker: Host
text_en: Sadly, we have become accustomed to living with the illegitimate use of force by government. To develop a truly free society, the issue of initiating force must be understood and rejected.
text: 令人遗憾的是，我们已经习惯了政府以不正当方式使用武力。要建立一个真正自由的社会，就必须理解并拒绝这种主动使用武力的做法。

speaker: Host
text_en: What if sometimes, to love your country, you had to alter or abolish the government? What if Jefferson was right? What if that government is best which governs least?
text: 如果有时候，爱你的国家就意味着你必须改革甚至废除政府呢？如果 Jefferson 是对的呢？如果最好的政府就是管得最少的政府呢？

speaker: Host
text_en: What if it is dangerous to be right when the government is wrong? What if it is better to perish fighting for freedom than to live as a slave? What if freedom's greatest hour of danger is now?
text: 如果当政府错了的时候，正确反而会变得危险呢？如果为自由而战、哪怕牺牲，也比像奴隶一样活着更好呢？如果自由面临的最大危险时刻就是现在呢？

speaker: Host
text_en: Hi everyone, Judge Andrew Napolitano here for Judging Freedom. Today is Tuesday, April 21st, 2026. Professor John Mearsheimer will be with us in just a moment on the U.S. is losing Israel's war. Who knows about that? But first this.
text: 大家好，我是 Andrew Napolitano 法官，欢迎收看 Judging Freedom。今天是 2026 年 4 月 21 日，星期二。John Mearsheimer 教授马上就会加入我们，聊聊“美国正在输掉 Israel 的战争”这个话题。到底是不是这样，谁知道呢？不过先看这个。

speaker: Host
text_en: Don't you just cringe when people say, I told you so? Sorry, I told you gold and silver would reap the benefits due to excessive money printing, inflation, and global uncertainty. It's here. It's happened. Gold and silver reached all-time highs.
text: 当别人说“我早就告诉过你了”的时候，你是不是也会觉得很刺耳？抱歉，我之前就说过，随着货币超发、通胀和全球不确定性加剧，黄金和白银会成为受益者。现在来了，真的发生了。黄金和白银都创下了历史新高。

speaker: Host
text_en: Did you call Lear Capital and buy some? It's not too late. Experts are predicting higher prices ahead. Why? Nothing has changed.
text: 你给 Lear Capital 打电话买一些了吗？现在还不晚。专家预测，接下来价格还会更高。为什么？因为根本什么都没变。

speaker: Host
text_en: Changed geopolitical chaos, cost of living crises and a weaker dollar are driving central banks to boost their gold reserves.
text: 地缘政治动荡、生活成本危机以及美元走弱，正推动各国央行增加黄金储备。

speaker: Host
text_en: Forecasts suggest gold could hit $6,000 an ounce and silver $200 an ounce. Even Morgan Stanley ditched the 60-40 rule for 60-20-20, putting 20% into precious metals. They're getting educated and you should too. Call the best in the business.
text: 预测显示，黄金可能涨到每盎司 6000 美元，白银可能到每盎司 200 美元。就连 Morgan Stanley 都放弃了 60-40 配置，改成了 60-20-20，把 20% 投进贵金属。他们已经开始补课了，你也该这样做。给业内最靠谱的人打电话。

speaker: Host
text_en: business and the people i trust lear capital get their reports get the facts get some gold and silver tell them the judge sent you and get up to twenty thousand dollars in bonus gold or silver call 800-511-4620 or go to learchudgenap.com professor john mearshammer good day to you my friend uh and welcome here were the negotiations
text: 我信得过的这家顶级公司就是 Lear Capital。拿他们的报告，了解事实，买点黄金和白银。告诉他们是 the judge 介绍你来的，你最高还能拿到价值两万美元的黄金或白银奖励。拨打 800-511-4620，或者访问 learchudgenap.com。Professor John Mearshammer，您好啊，我的朋友，欢迎您。上周美国和伊朗在 Islamabad 之间的谈判，是不是一场骗局？

speaker: Host
text_en: between the Americans and the Iranians in Islamabad last week, a hoax.
text: 上周美国和伊朗在 Islamabad 的谈判，是不是一场骗局？

speaker: Guest
text_en: I don't think it would be fair to call them a hoax. I'm not sure exactly what the Americans were doing. Beforehand, President Trump had indicated that he was going to negotiate on the basis of Iran's 10-point plan.
text: 我觉得把它们称作骗局并不公平。我也不太确定美国人当时到底在做什么。事先，Trump 总统曾表示，他会以伊朗提出的 10 点方案为基础进行谈判。

speaker: Guest
text_en: But then when the negotiations actually took place in Islamabad, what happened was that Vance reverted to the 15-point American plan. American plan, which had all the American maximalist demands in it. And unsurprisingly,
text: 但等到谈判真的在 Islamabad 进行时，结果却是 Vance 又回到了美国的 15 点方案。这个美国方案里包含了美国所有最强硬、要价最高的要求。毫不意外，

speaker: Guest
text_en: the Iranians had no interest in talking about those 15 demands that the Americans were making and ignoring the 10 demands that the Iranians were making. So those negotiations led nowhere. I'm not sure why we did that.
text: 伊朗人根本没兴趣讨论美国提出的那 15 项要求，同时还无视伊朗提出的 10 项要求。所以这些谈判毫无结果。我也不明白我们为什么要这么做。

speaker: Guest
text_en: I think you can understand why the Iranians came to. came to the table. They wanted to show the world that they were interested in negotiating a settlement to this conflict. But what the Americans were up to is hard to understand unless you believe.
text: 我觉得你能理解为什么伊朗人会来谈判桌前。他们想向全世界表明，他们有意通过谈判来解决这场冲突。但美国人到底在打什么算盘，就很难理解了，除非你相信，

speaker: Guest
text_en: that the Israelis intervened at some point along the line and they forced the Trump administration to move away from the 10-point plan to the 15-point plan.
text: 是 Israelis 在某个时候介入了，并迫使 Trump 政府从 10 点方案转向 15 点方案。

speaker: Guest
text_en: I think that's the most likely explanation for what was taking place then. In other words, it looked like Trump was willing to negotiate on the basis of Iranian demands, but then he switched gears.
text: 我觉得，这大概是当时最可能的解释。换句话说，当时看起来 Trump 愿意按照伊朗方面的要求来谈判，但后来他又突然转向了。

speaker: Guest
text_en: because the lobby in Israel itself put enormous pressure on him. That's possible as well.
text: 因为以色列内部的游说力量给了他巨大的压力。这种可能性也是有的。

speaker: Host
text_en: Right, right. In fact, even the either prime minister or chief of staff of the army, I forget which, of Pakistan.
text: 对，对。实际上，连巴基斯坦那边，应该是总理或者军队参谋长，我记不清具体是谁了。

speaker: Host
text_en: who was serving as the intermediary and the host had indicated that the 10-point plan was the basis preliminarily agreed to. It included a ceasefire in Lebanon, a total ceasefire in Lebanon, meaning not only against the Lebanese army,
text: 作为中间人和东道主的那一方曾表示，那份十点方案是双方初步同意的基础。其中包括黎巴嫩停火，全面停火，也就是说，不只是针对黎巴嫩军队停火，

speaker: Host
text_en: but against the Lebanese people and Hezbollah.
text: 还包括停止针对黎巴嫩人民和 Hezbollah 的行动。

speaker: Host
text_en: Trump probably didn't read it before he accepted it until he heard from his friend in Tel Aviv.
text: Trump 很可能是在接受之前根本没仔细看，直到他听到了自己那位 Tel Aviv 朋友的意见。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Yeah, I think that's right. I mean, I think it's just very important as we watch this whole situation evolve to understand that the Israelis and the lobby have a stranglehold on Trump and Trump has limited maneuver room.
text: 对，我觉得是这样。我的意思是，我们在看整个局势发展的时候，理解这一点非常重要：以色列和游说集团对 Trump 有很强的控制力，而 Trump 的回旋空间其实很有限。

speaker: Guest
text_en: He can't just do what he wants to do. He has to make sure that he checks with Tel Aviv or with Jerusalem. And he gets permission because if it begins to stray, enormous pressure is brought to bear on him. And up to now, he's been brought into line.
text: 他不能想做什么就做什么。他必须先跟 Tel Aviv 或者 Jerusalem 那边确认，得到许可，因为只要他开始偏离路线，就会有巨大的压力施加到他身上。而到目前为止，他一直都被拉回了既定轨道。

speaker: Host
text_en: The second round, which hasn't happened yet, I inadvertently misspoke yesterday when I said the vice president was in an Islamabad hotel.
text: 第二轮谈判还没发生。昨天我说副总统人在 Islamabad 的一家酒店里，那是我一时说错了。

speaker: Host
text_en: He apparently hasn't left D.C. because the Iranians say they don't want to negotiate. But why would they negotiate?
text: 显然他还没离开 D.C.，因为伊朗方面说他们不想谈判。但他们为什么要谈呢？

speaker: Host
text_en: with the U.S. when it just seized and heavily damaged one of its tankers, and when it plans to bring Netanyahu's minders with them again, the same two who concocted the subterfuge of June and February.
text: 跟美国谈吗？美国刚刚扣押并严重损坏了他们的一艘油轮，而且还打算再次把 Netanyahu 的那两个监护人带过去，就是那两个炮制了六月和二月那场骗局的人。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Well, again, I think the only reason the Iranians would do this is to show good faith. Unless something's happening in the back channels that indicates that the Americans are willing to make concessions.
text: 嗯，还是那句话，我觉得伊朗人这么做的唯一原因，就是为了表示善意。除非私下渠道里发生了什么，表明美国人愿意作出让步。

speaker: Guest
text_en: My view on this all along has been that the Iranians are in the driver's seat. And furthermore, the longer the war goes on and the more damage that's done to the international economy, the more leverage the Iranians have in these negotiations.
text: 我一直以来的看法都是，伊朗人现在掌握着主动权。而且，战争拖得越久，对国际经济造成的破坏越大，伊朗人在这些谈判中的筹码就越多。

speaker: Guest
text_en: If I had been playing their hand, I would not have.
text: 如果是我站在他们的立场上，我就不会这么做。

speaker: Guest
text_en: not have gone to Islamabad last week, and I certainly wouldn't be going now unless I had assurances from the Americans and from the Pakistanis that the Americans were actually willing to seriously negotiate on the basis of the 10-point plan.
text: 上周我就不会去 Islamabad，而且现在我肯定也不会去，除非我已经从美国人和巴基斯坦人那里得到保证，确认美国人确实愿意以那份十点方案为基础认真谈判。

speaker: Host
text_en: But again, I guess what they're trying to do here is just show good faith.
text: 但话说回来，我猜他们这么做也只是想表示善意。

speaker: Host
text_en: You know, I spoke with Muhammad Merandi, Professor Merandi, born in Alexandria.
text: 你知道，我和 Muhammad Merandi 聊过，Merandi 教授，出生在 Alexandria。

speaker: Guest
text_en: of virginia usa but a professor at the university of tehran and his family and children are his wife or iranian anyway he was part of the delegation that went to islamabad and he told me that they all thought they were going to be killed on the way back and as a result the pilot had to divert the last minute with no notice on the airwaves
text: Virginia, USA 的人，不过是 Tehran University 的教授，他的家人和孩子，还有他妻子都是 Iranian。总之，他是去 Islamabad 的那个代表团成员之一。他告诉我，他们当时都觉得自己在回程路上会被杀，所以最后飞行员不得不在最后一刻临时改道，而且没有通过无线电提前通知。

speaker: Guest
text_en: to another airport.
text: 飞去另一个机场。

speaker: Host
text_en: Who did they think was going to kill them?
text: 他们觉得是谁要杀他们？

speaker: Guest
text_en: Well, either the Israelis or the Americans, probably most likely the Israelis, because decapitation has been the order of the day for years now for the Israelis. They're constantly running around the Middle East, you know, killing leaders.
text: 嗯，要么是 Israelis，要么是 Americans，不过大概率还是 Israelis，因为这么多年来，斩首式清除一直就是 Israelis 的惯常做法。他们总是在中东到处行动，杀掉各路领导人。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And I think it was prudent on the part of the Iranians to fear for their life.
text: 所以我觉得，Iranians 为自己的性命担忧是很谨慎、很合理的。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Well, at that point, they said if there was a second round of negotiation, it would be in Moscow or Beijing where they would have no fear of attack. But I guess the again,
text: 当时他们说，如果还有第二轮谈判，那就会放在 Moscow 或 Beijing，这样他们就不用担心遭到袭击了。不过我想，还是又一次——

speaker: Host
text_en: I don't know who's running the show from Pakistan prime minister or head of the army prevailed. Who knows? Do you think there'll be another round?
text: 我也不知道 Pakistan 到底是谁在拍板，是总理还是军方首脑占了上风。谁知道呢？你觉得还会有下一轮吗？

speaker: Host
text_en: Before we leave this topic, you think there'll be another round of negotiation? I don't think Vance knew what he was talking about. Professor Morandi said every time they asked him a question that he could answer with a yes or no,
text: 在我们结束这个话题之前，你觉得还会有新一轮谈判吗？我觉得 Vance 根本不知道自己在说什么。Morandi 教授说，每次别人问他一个本来可以用“是”或“不是”来回答的问题时，

speaker: Host
text_en: what do you think he did? Got on the phone. And quote either Washington or Tel Aviv.
text: 你觉得他会怎么做？拿起电话，然后去问 Washington 或者 Tel Aviv。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Yeah, well, again,
text: 对，不过还是那句话，

speaker: Guest
text_en: I think what was going on here is that Vance's position during the negotiations, and I think even before the negotiations in Islamabad, hardened or stiffened, and it was in large part because the Israelis and the lobby itself were putting pressure on the United States and on Vance to demand a really.
text: 我觉得这里发生的情况是，Vance 在谈判期间的立场，甚至我认为在 Islamabad 的谈判开始之前，就已经变得更强硬、更僵化了，这在很大程度上是因为 Israelis 以及游说集团本身一直在向美国、向 Vance 施压，要求他们对伊朗提出一个非常强硬的协议。

speaker: Guest
text_en: tough deal with the Iranians and of course the Iranians would not go along so the whole process broke down but your question about where we're headed is a very interesting one I do think I wouldn't bet a lot of money on it because it's hard to
text: 当然，伊朗人不会接受，所以整个进程就破裂了。不过你问我们接下来会走向哪里，这个问题非常有意思。我确实认为——我不会在这上面下太大的赌注，因为很难

speaker: Guest
text_en: Be confident about any judgment you make when Trump is involved. But I would bet that negotiations do take place. I don't think the Americans, despite all the bluster from President Trump, have a vested interest in going up the escalation ladder.
text: 在 Trump 牵涉其中的时候，对任何判断都抱有十足把握。但我还是会押谈判会继续进行。我不认为美国人——尽管 Trump 总统一直在虚张声势——真的有既得利益去一步步升级局势。

speaker: Guest
text_en: I don't think we win.
text: 我不觉得那样我们会赢。

speaker: Guest
text_en: As we go up the escalation ladder, in fact, as I just said a few minutes ago, I think it's in Iran's interest to prolong the war. And if we go up the escalation ladder, it will work toward Iran's advantage over the long term.
text: 随着局势一步步升级，事实上，就像我几分钟前刚说的，我认为拖长这场战争符合伊朗的利益。而如果我们继续升级局势，从长期来看，反而会对伊朗更有利。

speaker: Guest
text_en: If you think about it, what are we going to do if we go up the escalation ladder? Are we going to use air power to pound Iran again? This is what President Trump is talking about. We don't have many military targets left to hit.
text: 你想想看，如果我们沿着升级的阶梯继续往上走，接下来要怎么办？难道还要再用空中力量去猛炸 Iran 吗？这就是 Trump 总统现在在说的事。可我们已经没剩下多少军事目标可打了。

speaker: Guest
text_en: So we'd have to hit civilian infrastructure. We'd have to hit power plants. We'd have to hit their energy systems and so forth and so on. And the end result.
text: 所以那就只能去打民用基础设施了。得去炸发电厂，得去打他们的能源系统，诸如此类。而最终结果——

speaker: Guest
text_en: fault is that they would retaliate. It's not like the other side has no ability to retaliate here. And they would retaliate by shutting the Red Sea, I believe, in addition to shutting down even further what's coming out of the Strait of Hormuz.
text: 问题在于，他们一定会报复。不是说对方在这件事上毫无还手之力。我认为他们会通过封锁红海来报复，除此之外，还会进一步切断经由 Hormuz 海峡运出的东西。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And furthermore, I think they would attack targets in Saudi Arabia and in the other Gulf states. And this would have disastrous consequences over the long term. for the world economy and therefore for the United States.
text: 而且，我觉得他们还会袭击 Saudi Arabia 以及其他海湾国家的目标。从长期来看，这会给世界经济带来灾难性后果，因此对美国也一样。

speaker: Guest
text_en: There's just no sort of air option here. And again, you want to remember that we have a limited number of boutique weapons that we've already used. And if we go back on the offensive with air power,
text: 这里其实根本没有什么空中打击选项。再说一遍，你得记住，我们手里那种数量有限的高端精确武器，之前已经用掉一些了。如果我们重新用空中力量转入进攻，

speaker: Guest
text_en: we would use up more of those missiles and boutique weapons. So there's no real air option here. And if you think about a ground option.
text: 就会进一步消耗那些导弹和高端武器。所以这里根本没有真正可行的空中选项。再想想地面选项的话——

speaker: Guest
text_en: we don't have a ground option there's just not enough combat troops in the region to have a serious ground option and furthermore even if we had a lot more ground forces going into Iran invading Iran with a land army would be remarkably foolish and wouldn't work and this by the way is why we're relying on the Navy today if you think about what's going on any military has three big instruments it can choose among one is its Navy two is its Air Force and
text: 我们也没有地面选项，这个地区根本没有足够的作战部队来支撑一个真正像样的地面方案。而且，哪怕我们有多得多的地面部队，派陆军进入 Iran、入侵 Iran 也是极其愚蠢的，而且根本行不通。顺便说一句，这也就是为什么我们今天依赖海军。你想想现在的局势，任何一支军队大体上都有三种主要工具可选：一种是海军，第二种是空军，

speaker: Guest
text_en: person three is its army. We have no army option here. We started the war with the air option by bombing. Iran. And that didn't work. And that's why we are where we are today. And what we've downturned to is the Navy option.
text: 第三种是陆军。我们在这里没有陆军选项。战争一开始，我们用的是空中选项，也就是轰炸 Iran。结果并没有奏效。所以我们今天才会走到这一步。而我们现在转向的，就是海军这个选项。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And this is what the blockade is all about. But that has no promise of ending this conflict on favorable terms. So you see, we have no military option here.
text: 而这正是封锁的核心所在。但这并不能保证以对我们有利的条件结束这场冲突。所以你看，我们在这里其实没有军事选项。

speaker: Host
text_en: Yeah.
text: 对。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And at the same time, there's the whole economic side of this story, which in many ways is more important than the military side of the story, because the damage that's being done to the to the world economy is great. And President Trump, therefore,
text: 与此同时，这件事还有完整的经济层面，而且很多方面比军事层面更重要，因为对世界经济造成的损害非常大。所以 President Trump 因此

speaker: Guest
text_en: has a deep-seated interest in settling this conflict immediately. I mean, he wants to settle it yesterday. And that's why I think the incentive to have some sort of negotiation is very great.
text: 有一种根深蒂固的利益诉求，要立刻解决这场冲突。我的意思是，他恨不得昨天就把它解决掉。所以我认为，推动某种谈判的动机是非常强的。

speaker: Guest
text_en: But the great problem the president faces is that he has to deal with the Israelis and the Israelis don't want a settlement. Very important to understand this. Trump wants a settlement. that Israel does not want to sell.
text: 但总统面临的最大问题是，他必须和 Israelis 打交道，而 Israelis 不想达成和解。这一点非常重要，一定要明白。Trump 想要和解，但 Israel 不想和解。

speaker: Host
text_en: Well, Israel can't accomplish anything without the U.S. If the United States enters into some serious ceasefire or truce, what are the Israelis going to do? Attack Iran on their own and risk the destruction of their country?
text: 可如果没有美国，Israel 什么也做不成。如果 United States 认真推动某种停火或休战，Israel 还能怎么办？自己去打 Iran，然后冒着国家被摧毁的风险吗？

speaker: Guest
text_en: No. Well, you want to remember here that the Israelis and the Americans together could not defeat Iran. If you're assessing who has won the war so far. It's very clear that the Iranians have won the war so far. None of our goals have been achieved.
text: 不会。你要记住，这里是 Israelis 和 Americans 联手都打不败 Iran。如果你要评估到目前为止是谁赢了这场战争，那很明显，到目前为止是 Iranians 赢了。我们的目标一个都没有实现。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And furthermore, we've lost control to Iran of the Strait of Hormuz. So we're worse off, we meaning the United States and the Israelis, today than we were before the war started. We just didn't achieve any goals.
text: 而且，我们还把 Hormuz Strait 的控制权丢给了 Iran。所以我们现在的处境更糟了——这里的“我们”指的是 United States 和 Israelis——比战争开始前还要糟。我们根本没有实现任何目标。

speaker: Guest
text_en: So if the United States and the Israelis together couldn't achieve the goals. The Israelis themselves can't do it. But what the Israelis want is they want us to stay in the fight.
text: 所以，如果美国和以色列联手都无法实现这些目标，那以色列自己更做不到。但以色列人想要的是，他们希望我们继续留在这场冲突里。

speaker: Guest
text_en: They want us to continue to hammer away at Iran and hopefully over a protracted period of time disable Iran to the point where it is greatly weakened. That's what they want. But what Trump wants is he wants to shut this one down as soon as possible. He knows that the damage that's being done to the international economy.
text: 他们希望我们继续不断打击 Iran，并且最好在一段较长时间里把 Iran 削弱到元气大伤的程度。这就是他们想要的。但 Trump 想要的是尽快把这件事收掉。他知道这正在对国际经济造成损害。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And the damage that might be done if this continues necessitates a settlement. But again, he's running up against the Israelis.
text: 而且如果这事继续下去，可能造成的进一步损害也意味着必须达成某种解决方案。但话说回来，他又在和以色列人硬碰硬。

speaker: Host
text_en: Here is Mike Waltz, the U.S. ambassador to Iran sounding very combative, sounding more like a politician than a diplomat, but that's his style.
text: 下面是 Mike Waltz，这位美国驻 Iran 大使，讲话时火药味很浓，听起来更像个政客而不是外交官，不过这就是他的风格。

speaker: Host
text_en: First is that you used the phrase earlier, and he uses it as well, escalate to de-escalate. And second is.
text: 第一，你刚才用了那个说法，他也用了，就是“升级是为了降级”。第二点是——

speaker: Host
text_en: This argument that destroying bridges and power plants is not a war crime. Chris, can you play these two back to back, first number three and then number four?
text: 这种说法认为，摧毁桥梁和发电厂不算战争罪。Chris，你能把这两段连着放一下吗？先放第三段，再放第四段。

speaker: Guest
text_en: All options are on the table. The president's prepared to escalate, to deescalate. He means it when he said nothing that benefits Iran is coming out of the straits. And then on top of that, Secretary Besson announced Operation Economic Fury,
text: 所有选项都摆在桌面上。总统已经准备好通过升级来实现降级。他说“任何对 Iran 有利的东西都别想从海峡运出去”这话是认真的。除此之外，Besson 部长还宣布了“经济怒火行动”。

speaker: Guest
text_en: where we are prepared to put secondary sanctions. on banks who are transacting in illegal Iranian oil dollars. So we are truly putting maximum pressure on every aspect of the Iranian economy.
text: 在这项行动中，我们准备对那些参与非法 Iran 石油美元交易的银行实施次级制裁。所以我们确实是在对 Iran 经济的方方面面施加最大压力。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And at some point, they are going to see some level of common sense and pragmatism and say enough is enough with this nuclear obsession. I hope we don't have to go back to a military option,
text: 到某个时候，他们总会表现出一些基本常识和务实态度，说对这种核执念已经受够了。我希望我们不用再回到军事选项上。

speaker: Guest
text_en: but President Trump's made it very clear. And by the way, bridges, power plants. that are run by the IRGC, which runs the entire military, are absolute legitimate military targets not only now but have been historically. That is a false, fake,
text: 但 President Trump 已经说得很清楚了。还有，像桥梁、发电厂这类由 IRGC 运营的设施——而 IRGC 掌控着整个军方——绝对是合法的军事打击目标，不只是现在，历史上也一直如此。把这说成是什么战争罪，这种说法是虚假的、编造的，

speaker: Guest
text_en: and ridiculous notion that this is some type of war crime.
text: 而且荒唐可笑。

speaker: Host
text_en: We're trying to threaten to attack civilian infrastructure. He should know that.
text: 我们这是在威胁要攻击民用基础设施。他应该知道这一点。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Yeah, I mean, what's going on here is that Walt thinks that we can punish Iran to the point where it will just throw up its hands and surrender. All we have to do is get tougher. And there are two problems with this argument.
text: 对，我的意思是，这里真正的问题在于，Walt 认为我们可以把 Iran 逼到一个地步，让它只能举手投降。我们要做的无非就是更强硬一点。而这个论点有两个问题。

speaker: Guest
text_en: The first is that we represent, we meaning the United States and Israel, represent an existential threat to Iran. And because we represent an existential threat. threat. They will fight to the last person.
text: 第一，我们——这里说的我们是指 United States 和 Israel——对 Iran 来说构成了生存性威胁。正因为我们构成了生存性威胁，他们会战斗到最后一个人。

speaker: Guest
text_en: They are not going to throw their hands up and surrender. They're going to fight like wild dogs. That's point number one. Point number two is that they have the advantage going up the escalation ladder.
text: 他们不会举手投降的。他们会像疯狗一样拼命打。这是第一点。第二点是，在升级阶梯上，他们反而更有优势。

speaker: Guest
text_en: People like Waltz don't understand that. The idea that we're going to go in there and we're going to bomb power plants in Iran and they're just going to sit there and take it and eventually throw up their hands. It's not a serious argument. They are going to respond and they have the ability to cause us enormous amounts of trouble.
text: 像 Waltz 这样的人不明白这一点。以为我们进去轰炸 Iran 的发电厂，他们就会坐在那里挨打，最后举手投降——这根本不是个严肃的论点。他们一定会反击，而且他们有能力给我们制造极大的麻烦。

speaker: Guest
text_en: The international economy is already in deep trouble. We are on the Titanic heading for an iceberg and something has to be done to shut this down. President Trump understands that. But if you up the ante, the Iranians will in turn up the ante.
text: 国际经济已经陷入很深的麻烦了。我们就像坐在 Titanic 上正朝着冰山撞过去，必须得采取措施把这件事叫停。President Trump 明白这一点。但如果你进一步加码，Iran 方面也会反过来加码。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And where does that leave us? It leaves us much worse off in terms of the international economy. And as I said at the start. We're not going to get them to surrender.
text: 那最后会把我们带到什么局面？就是让我们在国际经济层面处境更糟。正如我一开始说的，我们不可能逼他们投降。

speaker: Guest
text_en: So we have no option to escalate, to de-escalate. If we escalate, we are going to cause ourselves enormous amounts of trouble.
text: 所以我们没有升级的选项，只能降级处理。要是升级，我们会给自己带来巨大的麻烦。

speaker: Host
text_en: The Iranian ship that was stopped by the Navy, boarded by personnel, I don't know if they were Navy or Marines, they used their weapons to destroy the engine and then they stole the ship. It was a cargo ship, it wasn't a tanker, and it was empty.
text: 那艘被 Navy 拦下、由人员登船的 Iranian 船只——我不知道他们是 Navy 还是 Marines——他们用武器摧毁了发动机，然后把船给抢走了。那是一艘货船，不是油轮，而且是空船。

speaker: Host
text_en: What happens if they do that to a Chinese ship filled with three or four million barrels of oil?
text: 如果他们对一艘装着三四百万桶石油的 Chinese 船也这么干，会怎么样？

speaker: Guest
text_en: Well, the question that then comes up is, do the Chinese send military escorts? Do they send ships to escort those Chinese ships? Or do the Chinese put guns, man pads,
text: 那么接下来就会出现一个问题：Chinese 会不会派军事护航？他们会不会派军舰去护送那些 Chinese 船只？还是说 Chinese 会在那些船上部署枪支、单兵便携式防空导弹，

speaker: Guest
text_en: you know, and machine guns on those ships that can shoot down the helicopters that tried to board the ships? The Chinese have options here, and I don't think the Chinese will just stand idly by.
text: 你知道，还有机枪，好把那些试图登船的直升机打下来？Chinese 在这里是有选项的，我不认为 Chinese 会只是袖手旁观。

speaker: Guest
text_en: deadly by and let us get away with this over the long term. So I think there's a really serious risk of escalation here. And my guess would be if the Chinese send military escorts with these tankers or cargo ships or they arm them,
text: 袖手旁观，长期让我们就这么得逞。所以我认为这里确实存在非常严重的升级风险。我的猜测是，如果 Chinese 给这些油轮或货船派军事护航，或者直接把它们武装起来，

speaker: Guest
text_en: that the United States will back off. I don't think the United States wants to fight with the Chinese. But the longer this war goes on, the more you have a risk of the Chinese and even the Russians. becomes becoming involved.
text: 美国会退一步。我不认为美国想和中国开战。但这场战争拖得越久，中国、甚至俄罗斯卷入其中的风险就越大。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Because the Chinese and the Russians, it's important to emphasize this, have a vested interest in seeing Iran win this war.
text: 因为中国和俄罗斯——这一点很重要，必须强调——都切身希望看到 Iran 赢得这场战争。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And they are helping the Iranians. And once the war ends, they will continue to help the Iranians. Because what we have here is a situation where you have Iran, North Korea,
text: 而且他们正在帮助 Iran。等战争结束后，他们也会继续帮助 Iran。因为现在的局面是，Iran、North Korea、

speaker: Guest
text_en: China and Russia on one side and the United States and the Israelis on the other side.
text: China 和 Russia 在一边，美国和以色列在另一边。

speaker: Host
text_en: Have you seen any evidence of Israeli... recognition that it has failed. to achieve its military goals and is unlikely to achieve them. Same question with American recognition, not Trump, because he says things that are divorced from reality,
text: 你有没有看到任何迹象表明，以色列已经意识到自己失败了，没能实现军事目标，而且也不太可能实现？美国方面也是同样的问题，不是说 Trump，因为他说的话常常脱离现实，

speaker: Host
text_en: but American officialdom recognition.
text: 而是美国官方层面有没有这种认知。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Oh, I think that probably almost everybody who's in a decision making position in both the United States and Israel recognizes that up to this point we have failed to achieve our objectives. I mean, that's crystal clear.
text: 哦，我觉得在美国和以色列，几乎所有处在决策位置上的人，大概都意识到，到目前为止我们没能实现自己的目标。我的意思是，这一点再清楚不过了。

speaker: Guest
text_en: I think the interesting question is where do you go from here? And I think that almost all Israelis want to see the United States continue the war. They want us to see.
text: 我觉得有意思的问题是，接下来该怎么办？而且我认为，几乎所有以色列人都希望美国继续打这场战争。他们希望我们看到。

speaker: Guest
text_en: want to see us launch a massive bombing campaign along with the Israelis against Iran and continue to pummel away at that country.
text: 想看到我们和 Israelis 一起对 Iran 发动大规模轰炸行动，并且继续狠狠干那个国家。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Here in the United States, I would bet that most of President Trump's advisors would like to shut this war down and they see the Israelis as a huge problem. But then again,
text: 在美国这边，我敢说 President Trump 的大多数顾问都想把这场战争叫停，他们也把 Israelis 视为一个大麻烦。不过话又说回来，

speaker: Guest
text_en: as you know, there are all these Israeli assets who operate inside the U.S. government. government, people like Kushner, people like Steve Whitkoff, even people like Michael Waltz.
text: 你也知道，美国政府内部有不少替 Israel 办事的人。像 Kushner、Steve Whitkoff，甚至还有 Michael Waltz 这样的人。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And we refer to them more generally as neoconservatives, but neoconservatives is a euphemism for, you know, pro-Zionist forces inside the United States, and they basically follow the Israeli line.
text: 我们通常更笼统地把他们称作新保守主义者，但“新保守主义者”其实是一种委婉说法，说白了就是美国内部亲 Zionist 的力量，他们基本上就是跟着 Israel 的路线走。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And this is the problem that Trump faces. Trump is caught between two paths. powerful sets of forces. On one side, there is an imperative to shut this one down so as to stem the bleeding in terms of the international economy.
text: 而这就是 Trump 面临的问题。Trump 被夹在两股强大力量之间。一边是必须尽快把这场冲突压下去，好遏制国际经济层面的持续失血。

speaker: Guest
text_en: But on the other side are the Israelis and they're very powerful supporters here in the United States. And I think that is reflected in Mike Waltz's comments.
text: 但另一边是 Israelis，以及他们在美国这边非常有影响力的支持者。我觉得 Mike Waltz 的表态就反映了这一点。

speaker: Host
text_en: Does the United States engage in any serious diplomacy these days?
text: 如今美国还会认真搞外交吗？

speaker: Guest
text_en: Not really. And you see this, by the way, in the Ukraine case, right? Remember President Trump said that when he came into office, he would shut down the Ukraine Russia war overnight.
text: 其实并没有。顺便说一句，从 Ukraine 这个案例里你也能看出来，对吧？记得 President Trump 说过，他一上台就会在一夜之间叫停 Ukraine 和 Russia 的战争。

speaker: Guest
text_en: In fact, he promised or he said that there was a good chance he would shut it down before he even moved into the White House. It's been a total failure. And if you look at how we have behaved in the negotiating process, you just scratch your head.
text: 事实上，他承诺过，或者说他表示过，很有可能在他搬进白宫之前就把它关掉。这事彻底失败了。而且如果你看看我们在谈判过程中的表现，真的会一头雾水。

speaker: Guest
text_en: ahead and say, does the Trump administration have any understanding of how to conduct diplomacy? Sure doesn't look that way to me. This is what I see in the Ukraine-Russia case. And then if you look at what's going on with regard to Iran,
text: 你会忍不住直接问一句，Trump 政府到底懂不懂怎么搞外交？在我看来，显然是不懂。这就是我在 Ukraine-Russia 这个案例里看到的情况。再看看 Iran 那边发生的事，

speaker: Guest
text_en: it looks the same to me. It's very hard to figure out what the administration is up to. Just think about the ceasefire that was negotiated. There were two missing pieces in the ceasefire.
text: 在我看来也是一样。你很难搞清楚这个政府到底想干什么。就想想当时谈成的停火吧。那个停火里缺了两个关键环节。

speaker: Guest
text_en: One was we had to get the Israelis and Hezbollah to agree to a ceasefire. And then we had to get the Iranians to open the strait. These were the two final pieces of the puzzle just to get to a ceasefire.
text: 一个是我们得让 Israelis 和 Hezbollah 同意停火。然后还得让 Iranians 开放那条海峡。这是要达成停火前，拼图里最后的两块。

speaker: Guest
text_en: So on Friday, this is last Friday, I think it was April 17th, it looks like there's going to be a 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah. And then the Iranians say that they will open the Strait.
text: 所以到了周五，也就是上周五，我想应该是 4 月 17 日，看起来 Israel 和 Hezbollah 之间会有一个为期 10 天的停火。然后 Iranians 也表示他们会开放这条海峡。

speaker: Guest
text_en: This is on the 17th. And it looks by early morning on the 18th here in Chicago, like, you know, you have all of the ingredients of a ceasefire in place. But what does President Trump do?
text: 这是 17 号的情况。到了 18 号清晨，按 Chicago 这边的时间来看，感觉停火所需的一切条件都已经到位了。但 Trump 总统做了什么？

speaker: Guest
text_en: He refuses to take the American blockade off of the Strait of Hormuz and not surprisingly, the Iranians. And switch directions and put their blockade back on the Strait of Hormuz. And you just say to yourself, as you watch this,
text: 他拒绝解除美国对 Hormuz Strait 的封锁，而 Iranians 也毫不意外地立刻转向，重新对 Hormuz Strait 实施封锁。你看到这一幕时，心里只会想，

speaker: Guest
text_en: what is President Trump thinking? He wanted to get the ceasefire in place. He had the ceasefire in place.
text: Trump 总统到底在想什么？他本来是想把停火落实下来，而且当时停火其实已经到位了。

speaker: Guest
text_en: But common sense tells you that if you keep the blockade, the U.S. blockade on the Strait of Hormuz, which is a blockade on Iran, and you keep Iranian oil from...
text: 但常识告诉你，如果你继续维持封锁，也就是美国对 Hormuz 海峡的封锁——这实际上就是对 Iran 的封锁——并且不让 Iranian oil 从……

speaker: Guest
text_en: From coming through the strait that the Iranians are then going to put the blockade back on and you're back to square one.
text: 不让它通过这条海峡运出来，那 Iranians 接下来就会重新实施封锁，事情又回到原点了。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And the price of gasoline at the pump in the U.S. will continue to rise.
text: 而且美国加油站的汽油价格还会继续上涨。

speaker: Host
text_en: Yeah, exactly. That's the other key dimension to this. We had been allowing Iranian oil out into the global market because it was in our interest for the purposes of keeping oil prices down. But if you keep the American blockade of Iran on.
text: 对，没错。这是这件事的另一个关键维度。我们之前之所以允许 Iranian oil 进入全球市场，是因为这符合我们的利益，目的是压低油价。但如果你继续维持美国对 Iran 的封锁。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Then that Iranian oil can't get out into global markets. This just doesn't make any sense at all. And furthermore, if you're going to have a ceasefire, if you're going to have negotiations.
text: 那 Iranian oil 就进不了全球市场。这整件事根本毫无道理。而且更进一步说，如果你要停火，如果你要谈判。

speaker: Guest
text_en: this week right wouldn't it make sense to show some goodwill by taking the blockade off of Iran but no that's not what we did and I just scratch my head and say I don't understand what the strategy is here I can't tell myself a plausible strategy as to why this makes sense
text: 就是这周，对吧？那表现出一点善意、解除对 Iran 的封锁，不是更合理吗？但没有，我们并没有这么做。我只能一头雾水地说，我完全不明白这里的策略是什么。我根本想不出一个说得通的策略，来解释为什么这件事有道理。

speaker: Host
text_en: Here's Congressman Khanna of California.
text: 下面是 California 的国会议员 Khanna。

speaker: Host
text_en: sort of mocking this Mike Walsh theory of escalate to de-escalate. Chris, cut number 11.
text: 他算是在嘲讽 Mike Walsh 那套“通过升级来降级”的理论。Chris，放第 11 段。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Well, they said they want to escalate to de-escalate. They've escalated to devastation. I mean, you have the pope lecturing America about possible war crimes. You have the president, as you pointed out, threatening to destroy all power plants.
text: 嗯，他们说是要通过升级来实现降级。结果他们把局势升级成了毁灭性的局面。我的意思是，连教皇都在就可能的战争罪指责美国。还有总统，正如你指出的那样，威胁要摧毁所有发电厂。

speaker: Guest
text_en: I didn't think we would ever get to that point. You have the Strait of Hormuz that is now blocked. Block, this never happened before the war. What have we achieved? Gas is up from $2.30 to $4. You have now Iran having a more hardline regime,
text: 我真没想到事情会走到这一步。现在霍尔木兹海峡已经被封锁了。封锁，这在战前从来没发生过。我们到底取得了什么？油价已经从2.30美元涨到了4美元。现在伊朗也出现了一个更强硬的政权，

speaker: Guest
text_en: as we just heard, and all our allies like UAE being hit. I mean, we've created devastation and we're being lectured by the Pope.
text: 就像我们刚刚听到的那样，而且我们所有的盟友，像 UAE，也都在遭受打击。我的意思是，我们制造了一场浩劫，还要被教皇训话。

speaker: Host
text_en: There you have it.
text: 情况就是这样。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Hard to disagree. Rokano was my student, by the way, when he was an undergraduate at the University of Chicago.
text: 很难不同意。顺便说一句，Rokano 当年在 University of Chicago 读本科时，还是我的学生。

speaker: Host
text_en: Oh, for goodness sakes. Well, he learned well.
text: 哦，天哪。看来他学得不错。

speaker: Guest
text_en: I'm not sure I had that much influence, but it seems to me that everything he said made eminently good sense.
text: 我不确定我有那么大的影响，但在我看来，他说的每一句话都非常有道理。

speaker: Host
text_en: Yeah, here's more good sense. This is Tucker Carlson lamenting with his brother Buckley Carlson about the aid that they gave President Trump when he was running in 2024.
text: 对，下面还有更多有道理的话。这是 Tucker Carlson 和他弟弟 Buckley Carlson 在感叹，他们在 Trump 竞选 2024 年总统时给过他的那些帮助。

speaker: Host
text_en: This is sad, but my hat is off to his intellectuals. Honestly, the brother only gets one word in. This is a Tucker lament, and my hat is off to him. Chris number 13.
text: 这让人挺难过的，但我真得向他的理性和见识致敬。说实话，那位老兄总共也就插进来一句话。这就是 Tucker 的一段哀叹，我向他致敬。Chris，13号片段。

speaker: Guest
text_en: You and I and everyone else who supported him, you wrote speeches for him, I campaigned for him. We're implicated in this for sure.
text: 你、我，还有所有支持过他的人——你给他写过演讲稿，我替他拉过票。这里面肯定都有我们的一份责任。

speaker: Guest
text_en: It's not enough to say, well, I changed my mind or like, oh, this is bad, I'm out.
text: 光说一句“嗯，我改主意了”，或者“哦，这太糟了，我退出”，这还远远不够。

speaker: Guest
text_en: it's like in very small ways but in real ways you and me and millions of people like us for the reason this is happening right now so i do think it's like a moment to wrestle with our own consciences uh you know we'll be tormented by it for a long time i will be and and i want to say i'm sorry for misleading people in it was not intentional that's all
text: 虽然只是一些很小的方式，但又是实实在在的方式，你、我，还有几百万个像我们这样的人，都是眼下这一切会发生的原因之一。所以我确实觉得，这是一个该认真拷问我们自己良心的时刻。你知道，这件事会折磨我们很久，我自己就会。而且我想说一句，对不起，我误导了大家，虽然那并非有意为之。就这些。

speaker: Host
text_en: Well, look, when he campaigned for Trump, Trump rejected the neocon mantra, said he would never do just what he now has done. This is not Tucker Carlson's fault, but I salute him for this emotional intellectual honesty.
text: 你看，他当初为 Trump 助选时，Trump 是明确拒绝新保守主义那套口号的，还说自己绝不会去做如今他已经做了的这些事。这不是 Tucker Carlson 的错，但我还是要向他这种带着情感的理性诚实致敬。

speaker: Guest
text_en: I would just say I did not vote for Trump and am not a Trump supporter in any way, but I thought when he came in, given everything that he had said during the campaign,
text: 我只想说，我没有投票给 Trump，也绝不是 Trump 的支持者，但我原本以为他上台之后，考虑到他在竞选期间说过的那些话，

speaker: Guest
text_en: that he would not behave at all the way he has behaved on the foreign policy front. And I think everybody who is on your show, all the regular guests you have, had a lot of goodwill towards President Trump on the foreign policy front.
text: 他在外交政策上完全不会表现成现在这个样子。而且我觉得，上你节目的人，包括你那些常驻嘉宾，在外交政策方面，原本都对 Trump 总统抱有不少善意。

speaker: Guest
text_en: soon after he moved into the White House.
text: 尤其是在他刚搬进白宫后不久。

speaker: Host
text_en: Yes, because we believed what he said.
text: 对，因为我们相信了他说的话。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Yeah, I think that's exactly right. But what happens here is that the lobby eventually goes to work on all of our presidents, all of our leaders, and they basically coerce them into towing the line.
text: 对，我觉得这说得完全没错。但这里发生的情况是，这个游说集团最终会对我们所有总统、所有领导人施加影响，基本上就是逼他们按既定路线走。

speaker: Guest
text_en: And this gets back to the earlier conversation we had about what happened before the Islamabad negotiation. You remember it looked like Trump was going to negotiate on the basis of the Iranian 10-point plan,
text: 这也回到了我们之前谈到的、Islamabad 谈判前发生的事情。你还记得，当时看起来 Trump 是要以伊朗提出的十点方案为基础来谈判的，

speaker: Guest
text_en: and then that all went out the window. And then the reports are that when J.D. Vance was actually doing the negotiating in Islamabad, that his position stiffened during the negotiations as he talked to Netanyahu.
text: 结果后来这一切全都泡汤了。然后有报道称，当时 J.D. Vance 在 Islamabad 真正参与谈判时，他在谈判过程中的立场变得更强硬了，因为他一直在和 Netanyahu 通话。

speaker: Guest
text_en: and talk to Trump and company who were obviously talking to Netanyahu as well. So you see the tremendous power that the lobby has on our leaders, and this is what happened to President Trump. And Tucker,
text: 也在和 Trump 以及他身边的人沟通，而他们显然也同样在和 Netanyahu 交流。所以你能看出这个游说集团对我们领导人有多么巨大的影响力，这也正是 Trump 总统身上发生的事。至于 Tucker，

speaker: Guest
text_en: of course, understands that completely because he now fully understands just how powerful the lobby is, and he fully understands what you and I do, that Israel and the lobby are a giant albatross. Arbitrage around their neck.
text: 他当然完全明白这一点，因为他现在已经彻底明白这个游说集团到底有多强大了，也完全明白你我都清楚的那件事：Israel 和这个游说集团就是一个沉重的大包袱，死死压在他们脖子上。

speaker: Host
text_en: Professor Mersheimer, thank you very much, my dear friend. Great analysis.
text: Mersheimer 教授，非常感谢你，我亲爱的朋友。分析得太精彩了。

speaker: Host
text_en: As always, all my best to you. We'll look forward to seeing you next week.
text: 一如既往，向你致以最美好的祝愿。期待下周再见。

speaker: Guest
text_en: Likewise, Judge.
text: 彼此彼此，Judge。

speaker: Host
text_en: Coming up, if you're watching us live in 29 minutes at 10 o'clock this morning, Colonel Bill Astori at one this afternoon, the golden boy, Max Blumenthal at two this afternoon, Matthew Ho at three this afternoon, Colonel Karen Kwiatkowski,
text: 接下来，如果你现在在看我们的直播，再过 29 分钟，也就是今天早上 10 点，Colonel Bill Astori 会来；今天下午 1 点是那位金童 Max Blumenthal；今天下午 2 点是 Matthew Ho；今天下午 3 点是 Colonel Karen Kwiatkowski，

speaker: Host
text_en: Judge Napolitano for judging freedom.
text: 这里是 Judge Napolitano，捍卫自由。
